meta.title: Emergency Center
meta.description: Central hub for information and aid during national emergencies in Venezuela. Collection centers, shelters, hospitals, official agencies and more.
meta.tagline: Verified information. Centralized aid. United community.

devNotice.message: We are still actively developing and updating information on an ongoing basis, adding more features over time. Thank you for your patience.

communityFeedback.voteHint: Anyone can vote, with or without an account. Your vote helps the community prioritize reliable information.
communityFeedback.commentHint: You can comment with or without an account. If not signed in, you may add an optional display name.
communityFeedback.commentPlaceholder: Share additional information, corrections or your experience…

banner.title: Seismic emergency in Venezuela
banner.subtitle: Earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 on June 24, 2026. State of emergency active. Find verified aid in one place.

about.title: A place to find and give verified help
about.lead: Emergency Center brings together the essential information to respond to the Venezuela earthquake: where to donate, where to shelter, which hospitals are operating, who to look for and how to offer your help. All community-verified and without duplicating tools that already exist.
about.mission: Save time during an emergency. Instead of searching across dozens of scattered accounts, groups and links, we centralize reliable information and let the community validate it in real time.

points.centralizeDesc: Collection centers, shelters, hospitals, official agencies and solidarity companies in a single map and search.
points.verifyDesc: Anyone can vote on how truthful each item is and leave comments, with or without an account.
points.connectTitle: We connect help with those who need it
points.connectDesc: Missing person reports, volunteer registration and routes to the nearest aid points.
points.openDesc: Free and open-source platform. No sign-up needed to browse, donate or report.

footprints.title: All sections
footprints.subtitle: Every module of the platform in one place for quick access.

quickAccess.title: Quick access
quickAccess.subtitle: Jump straight to the section you need.
quickAccess.missingDesc: Hybrid hub: links, source summary and backup registry
quickAccess.volunteerDesc: Register or join as rescuer for Caracas/La Guaira
quickAccess.newsDesc: Publish, vote and assess news with the community
quickAccess.resourcesDesc: What to do before, during and after

situation.title: Situation summary
situation.content: On June 24, 2026, two earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 affected north-central Venezuela. The government declared a state of emergency and activated a Contingency Joint Command. Hundreds of casualties, structural damage in Caracas and La Guaira, service disruptions and communication outages have been reported. This platform centralizes verified information without duplicating existing tools.

external.title: Useful platforms and tools
external.venapp: VenApp — Report missing persons and damage

helpCenters.title: Help centers
helpCenters.subtitle: Verified collection centers for water, food, medicine and more.

cargoBanner.title: Need to take your donations to a collection center in Barquisimeto?
cargoBanner.description: There are free rides to move supplies to the collection centers in Barquisimeto. Contacts: Ronny Oviedo and Gabriela Arroyo.

logistics.title: Vamos App enabled free rides for donations
logistics.description: If you need to take donations to a collection center, open Vamos App and select the Donations option. The ride appears with a $0.00 cost to support aid delivery.

needs.title: What to donate or buy
needs.subtitle: Medical supplies requested by care teams. You can purchase these and take them to any collection center listed below.
needs.disclaimer: Community-shared reference list. Each center may have different priorities; confirm at the drop-off point before bringing large quantities.

items.nifedipine: Nifedipine tabs 10 mg and drops 20 mg

scaytaller.title: Filament donations for 3D-printed splints
scaytaller.description: The @scaytaller collective 3D-prints splints for injured people and is requesting filament donations to keep producing. Contact them on Instagram or by phone to coordinate delivery.

register.title: Register collection center
register.subtitle: Publish your drop-off point or report one you know so more people can donate.
register.modeOwnDesc: I coordinate or represent this site. You need an account to manage volunteers and inventory in the panel.
register.modeThirdDesc: You know another drop-off point and want it on the map. No account required.
register.modeOwnNotice: To register your own center you must sign up or sign in. Once published you will be assigned as coordinator and can access the panel.
register.authSubtitle: Create an account or sign in to continue registering your center.
register.authConfirmEmail: Check your email to confirm your account, then sign in again.
register.authError: Could not authenticate. Check your credentials.
register.authRequired: You must sign in to register your center.
register.thirdPartyNotice: This listing will be pending community verification before showing as confirmed.
register.photoHint: Upload a photo of the front or collection area (JPG, PNG or WebP, max 5 MB).
register.photoRequired: You must upload a center photo to continue.
register.description: Description (what they accept, where supplies go, etc.)
register.consent: I confirm this information is accurate and authorize its publication for aid coordination
register.successThirdParty: Center reported. It will appear in the list for community verification.
register.submitError: Could not submit registration. Please try again.

hub.heroTitle: Collection center and solidarity support network
hub.heroSubtitle: Register and look up aid points, care centers and free delivery volunteers in real time across affected areas of Venezuela.
hub.criticalBody: To optimize logistics, you must contact the center directly before moving any supplies. This ensures you bring what is actually needed today and that the site is active and receiving donations.

centers.title: Collection centers
centers.description: Browse or register active locations consolidating water, non-perishable food, medicine and clothing for communities.

delivery.title: Free delivery
delivery.description: Browse or join the network of vans, cars and volunteer motorcyclists who transport humanitarian aid at no cost to critical points.

hub.directorySubtitle: Filter by city and sector to find available aid immediately.
hub.noResults: No centers match the applied filters.
hub.deliveryTabIntro: Solidarity transport network to carry donations to critical points. Confirm availability with the volunteer before coordinating the trip.
hub.deliveryNoResults: No delivery volunteers match the filters. Check centroacopio.site or offer your vehicle.

alliedPlatform.title: Centros de Acopio Venezuela
alliedPlatform.description: centroacopio.site hosts community registrations of collection centers and drivers across several cities. Use it to complement this directory or register there if you already participate in that network.
alliedPlatform.note: Data syncs independently; always verify with the center before moving supplies.

detail.title: Center details
detail.emptyInventory: This center has not published inventory yet.
detail.staffNeededEmpty: Contact the center for more details.

activeAcopio.title: Active collection centers in Venezuela
activeAcopio.subtitle: Community list from June 25, 2026 with verified drop-off points across several regions. Check what to bring and the nearest site before transporting donations.
activeAcopio.virtualNote: Virtual support — no physical drop-off site
activeAcopio.disclaimer: Confirm with the center that it is still active and what supplies are prioritized today. Information from @rdelbufalo and may change.

g3Caritas.title: Where to donate — G3 Logística and Caritas
g3Caritas.subtitle: Drop-off points for supplies, materials and donations in Caracas, Valencia and Barquisimeto (G3), plus the Caritas Venezuela Montalbán headquarters.
g3Caritas.disclaimer: G3 Logística: Mon–Fri 9:00 AM–12:00 PM and 2:00–3:30 PM. Confirm hours and availability before bringing donations.

hospitals.title: Hospitals and clinics
hospitals.subtitle: National directory of hospitals and clinics in Venezuela with addresses, map and state filters.
hospitals.socialFallback: If a hospital website is down, check the Ministry of Health social accounts for updates.

hub.searchPlaceholder: Search hospital by name, city or address

admissionsRegistry.eyebrow: Looking for a relative who was admitted?
admissionsRegistry.title: Hospitales en Venezuela
admissionsRegistry.description: This allied platform digitizes lists of people admitted to hospitals and health centers. Search by full name or national ID to see if they are registered and at which facility. For privacy it does not show clinical status — call the hospital to confirm.
admissionsRegistry.note: If the person is not listed at any hospital, they may be missing — also check the missing persons registry.

shelters.title: Shelters
shelters.subtitle: Temporary shelters with available services and reported capacity.

damage.title: Damage map
damage.subtitle: Buildings and structures affected by the earthquake in Venezuela — synced data and community reports.
damage.sourceCredit: Data imported and progressively verified from the community damage map
damage.disclaimer: Community-provided information under validation. Always verify with authorities. In an emergency, call 171.
damage.empty: No buildings match your search. Try other filters or report a new one.
damage.reportSubtitle: Share the location of an affected building or structure. No account needed.

hub.progressBanner: Figures in progress: these numbers are being verified and validated from user-submitted information. They may change as we confirm each report.

engineersPlatform.title: Ingenieros por Venezuela
engineersPlatform.subtitle: Independent civil response initiative after the June 24, 2026 earthquake. Civil and structural engineers can register as volunteers; citizens can report damaged buildings to prioritize inspections.

priorityRescue.title: Priority search and rescue sites
priorityRescue.subtitle: Critical structures and zones with reported victims after the June 24, 2026 earthquakes. GPS coordinates for rescue teams.
priorityRescue.disclaimer: Community-shared information (@rdelbufalo / Terremoto Venezuela). Verify with authorities and official teams before deploying. In an emergency, call 171.

form.notice: Only report information you know. Do not post sensitive data. Verify before sharing.
form.sourcePlaceholder: E.g.: Terremoto Venezuela, outlet or social media
form.consent: I declare the information is truthful and authorize its publication for humanitarian purposes.

agencies.title: Official agencies
agencies.subtitle: Verified links to Civil Protection, Firefighters, Red Cross, Police and local governments.
agencies.socialFallback: Several official websites are unstable or down. Use social media as an alternative information channel.
agencies.redCrossFamilyLink: Family contact restoration → see Missing persons

missing.title: Missing persons
missing.subtitle: Deduplicated unified registry from citizen platforms, with links to official sources and a backup form.
missing.hubQuote: Deduplicated unified registry: we import from citizen platforms and show each person only once.
missing.hubQuoteDetail: We also link official sources and keep a backup form when a platform doesn't cover a case.
missing.disclaimer: To report or search for someone, use the linked platforms below first. VenApp is the official government channel.

redCross.title: Red Cross — Family contact restoration
redCross.quote: In an emergency, the first thing we think about is our loved ones. That's normal. That's human.
redCross.description: The Venezuelan Red Cross has activated its Family Contact Restoration service. If you lost communication with a family member and need to know if they are safe, contact us.

venevision.title: Venevisión — Missing persons reporting line
venevision.description: Venevisión has activated a line to report missing persons and broadcast them on the channel's screens.

external.title: External platforms
external.subtitle: Direct links to official, community and NGO registries. Use these as your first option.

aggregator.title: Source summary
aggregator.subtitle: Figures published by each platform. The same person may appear on several — do not add up totals.
aggregator.disclaimer: These figures come from each website and are updated manually. They do not represent unique people: many families report on more than one platform. To report or search, use each source's direct link.
aggregator.noSumWarning: Do not sum figures across platforms: people are duplicated between registries.

official.title: Official balance (authorities)
official.subtitle: Figures reported by the national government on June 25, 2026. Different from citizen platform counts.

petsCrosslink.title: Looking for a lost pet?
petsCrosslink.description: This section is for missing people. If you lost your dog or cat in the earthquake, Academia Leguau accepts reports via Instagram and WhatsApp (not a website).

hospitalsCrosslink.title: Looking for someone admitted to a hospital?
hospitalsCrosslink.description: Hospitales en Venezuela digitizes admission lists from health centers. Search by name or ID to find which hospital has registered the person.

backup.title: Backup registration
backup.subtitle: Before registering here, check if the person is already listed on the main platforms.

registry.title: Deduplicated unified registry
registry.subtitle: We consolidate records from allied platforms that register missing persons (Venezuela Te Busca, Encuéntralos, Venezuela Reporta, Terremoto Venezuela and Desaparecidos Terremoto). Each person appears once with links to every source. National ID helps prevent duplicates.
registry.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, ID or location…
registry.statsDedupNote: Deduplicated count — do not add up totals from each platform.

form.nationalIdHint: Optional but recommended: helps us match the same person across platforms and avoid duplicates.
form.photoHint: Stored in Supabase Storage. Max 8 MB.
form.hasExternalLink: This person is also registered on another platform
form.backupNotice: Before registering here, check if they are already on VenApp, Venezuela Te Busca or other main platforms. This form serves as backup and centralization. If the person already exists in our registry, no duplicate will be created.
form.duplicateError: This person is already in our registry. No duplicate was created.
form.consent: I confirm the information is accurate and authorize its use for search coordination

venapp.name: VenApp
venapp.desc: Official government platform to report missing persons and housing damage.

desaparecidosTerremoto.name: Desaparecidos Terremoto Venezuela
desaparecidosTerremoto.desc: Voluntary citizen tool to report and search for people after the earthquake. Includes public listings and emergency numbers.

venezuelaTeBusca.name: Venezuela Te Busca
venezuelaTeBusca.desc: Citizen initiative to reconnect families and help find missing persons.

pets.title: Lost pets
pets.subtitle: Links to community platforms and social media to report and search for lost pets.
pets.disclaimer: Unified listing from HuellasCAN (earthquake) plus community resources. To report a new pet, use the HuellasCAN button above.

registry.title: Lost and found pets registry
registry.subtitle: Synced listing from HuellasCAN — Earthquake. Search by name, area or state.
registry.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, area or markings…

academiaLeguau.title: Did you lose your pet in the earthquake?
academiaLeguau.subtitle: Registration via Instagram and WhatsApp — Academia Leguau
academiaLeguau.body: This is not a website: it's the @academialeguau Instagram account. Message them on WhatsApp or DM with your pet's photo and the requested details.
academiaLeguau.notWebsiteNote: Community channel on social media, not a standalone web platform.

vetcity.title: They also deserve medical attention
vetcity.subtitle: VETCITY — Maracay
vetcity.body: Many animals were affected during the earthquake. At VETCITY they receive affected pets to provide solidarity medical care.

huellascan.name: HuellasCAN — Earthquake
huellascan.desc: Public emergency platform to reunite lost dogs and cats after the earthquake. No sign-up or passwords required.

news.title: News & community fact-checking
news.subtitle: Publish news, vote whether it seems true or false, and see what information the community finds most credible.

feed.disclaimer: Votes help prioritize information but do not replace official verification. Always check reliable sources before sharing.
feed.voteError: Could not register your vote. Please try again.

form.sectionSubtitle: Share a link or summary so the community can assess its credibility.
form.title: Headline
form.summaryPlaceholder: Summarize what the story is about and why it matters for the emergency.
form.hint: The item will appear as a community contribution until an editor verifies it.
form.success: News published. The community can vote now.
form.error: Could not publish the news item. Please try again.

volunteers.title: Volunteer registration
volunteers.subtitle: Offer your professional or logistical help during the emergency.

lodging.title: Free lodging in Tucacas for people coming to help
lodging.description: Club de Playa Calipso (Tucacas, Falcón) offers free lodging for people traveling to support the emergency response. Share the word and coordinate before arriving.

rescuerCall.title: Call for rescuers and civil defense
rescuerCall.subtitle: Do you know someone who wants to help in Caracas or La Guaira?
rescuerCall.body: If you know a rescuer or civil defense member in Barquisimeto, Yaracuy or Lara who wants to support efforts in Caracas or La Guaira but cannot afford to travel, this initiative will provide transportation and meal expenses.
rescuerCall.urgency: They need our support in Caracas and La Guaira. The first 72 hours are critical for people still trapped under rubble.
rescuerCall.targetRegions: Rescuers or civil defense from Barquisimeto, Yaracuy and Lara
rescuerCall.offerText: Transportation to the emergency zone and meal expenses
rescuerCall.share: Please share this call with anyone who can help.
rescuerCall.formSubtitle: If you offer other types of help (medical, logistics, etc.), fill out the form below.

laraTransportCall.title: Free transport Lara → Caracas
laraTransportCall.subtitle: Are you civil protection, firefighter, park ranger or rescue in Lara?
laraTransportCall.body: Civil protection, firefighters, park rangers and rescue groups from Lara heading to support Caracas can get in touch — a vehicle is available for free transportation.
laraTransportCall.urgency: Leaving today — contact as soon as possible if you can join support efforts in Caracas.
laraTransportCall.targetRegions: Civil protection, firefighters, park rangers and rescue groups from Lara
laraTransportCall.share: Share with emergency teams in Lara who want to join.

medicalCall.title: Call for medical team to Caracas
medicalCall.subtitle: Medical students, doctors, paramedics and nurses
medicalCall.body: A care and rescue team is being formed to travel to Caracas with the Seguro Pastor Oropeza personnel and help affected people.
medicalCall.urgency: Leaving today — if you are willing to travel and support, contact Dr. Héctor as soon as possible.
medicalCall.targetRegions: Medical students, doctors, paramedics and nurses
medicalCall.offerText: Care and rescue team with Seguro Pastor Oropeza personnel
medicalCall.share: Share with medical personnel and students who can travel to Caracas.

unefmTucacasCall.title: UNEFM volunteering — Tucacas needs us!
unefmTucacasCall.subtitle: Medical students from Universidad Nacional Experimental Francisco de Miranda
unefmTucacasCall.body: Following the national earthquake emergency, UNEFM medical students are mobilizing to provide medical care and aid to those affected in Tucacas.
unefmTucacasCall.urgency: Mobilization on Friday, June 25. No donation is too small when it comes to saving lives!
unefmTucacasCall.targetRegions: UNEFM medical students, volunteers and people who can donate supplies
unefmTucacasCall.offerText: Sterile gauze, alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, acetaminophen, ibuprofen, oral rehydration salts, water, gloves and non-perishable food
unefmTucacasCall.share: Share with medical students, volunteers and donors in Coro and Falcón.

acariguaTransportCall.title: Transport needed: Acarigua → La Guaira
acariguaTransportCall.subtitle: Nursing staff willing to support in affected areas
acariguaTransportCall.body: A group of trained health personnel (nursing) wants to go to La Guaira to help in affected areas, but they do not have transportation from Acarigua.
acariguaTransportCall.urgency: Urgent — if you can offer a vehicle or coordinate the trip, contact as soon as possible.
acariguaTransportCall.targetRegions: Trained nursing staff willing to support in La Guaira
acariguaTransportCall.share: Help us spread the word! If you have a vehicle or know someone who can help.

cargoTransportCall.title: Free rides to take supplies to collection centers
cargoTransportCall.subtitle: Free transportation within Barquisimeto
cargoTransportCall.body: They offer free rides to move supplies or whatever you need to the collection centers in Barquisimeto. Contacts: Ronny Oviedo (0424-510-03-13) and Gabriela Arroyo (0412-892-61-26).
cargoTransportCall.urgency: Available to support donation logistics in Barquisimeto.
cargoTransportCall.targetRegions: People who need to move supplies to collection centers in Barquisimeto
cargoTransportCall.offerText: Free transportation of supplies to collection centers
cargoTransportCall.share: Share with anyone who has donations to take to the collection centers in Barquisimeto.

redCrossCall.title: Join as a Venezuelan Red Cross volunteer
redCrossCall.body: We have opened a registry for those who wish to support the response following the seismic events recorded on June 24. We are prioritizing volunteers in health and emergency response; however, every bit of help counts.
redCrossCall.priorityText: Health and emergency response volunteers (all help is welcome)
redCrossCall.share: Official call from the Venezuelan Red Cross.

interpretersCall.title: Interpreters needed for international medical aid
interpretersCall.body: Allied platform connecting interpreters with international rescue brigades. Register with your languages, location and availability. There is also a WhatsApp group for additional coordination.
interpretersCall.priorityText: Interpreters and professionals from all fields — not only medicine and engineering
interpretersCall.share: Share with bilingual or multilingual people who can support coordination.

resources.subtitle: What to do before, during and after an emergency.

offlineTools.title: Offline tools
offlineTools.subtitle: Useful apps when there is no signal, mobile data or stable power. The chat does not rely on the internet.

psychologicalSupport.title: Psychological support
psychologicalSupport.subtitle: Psychological first aid, emotional containment and crisis intervention for people affected by the emergency.

search.subtitle: Find help centers, shelters, hospitals, missing persons, emergency numbers and more.

search.hint: Search by city, service, name or type of aid. Shortcut: Ctrl+K

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect information on Emergency Center.

sections.title: 1. Data controller
paragraphs: Emergency Center is a free and open-source platform operated by StartupVen (startupven.com), with contributions from Zackness (pages.zackness.dev). This policy describes how personal data is processed when you use the website and its forms.
By submitting information or creating an account, you confirm that you have read this policy.

sections.title: 2. Data we may collect
paragraphs: Depending on how you use the platform, we may process:
list: Technical browsing data (IP address, browser type, pages visited, language).

Data you voluntarily enter in forms: name, phone, email, location, descriptions and other fields depending on the module (volunteers, help centers, missing persons, damage reports, news, companies, etc.).
Login credentials if you create a coordinator or administrator account.
Community votes, comments and ratings (may include an anonymous browser identifier to prevent duplicates).
Local preferences: light/dark theme, language and session tokens stored in cookies or browser storage.

sections.title: 3. Purpose of processing
paragraphs: We use data to:
list: Publish and display aid information during emergencies (centers, shelters, reports, etc.).
Enable registration of volunteers, solidarity companies and help center coordinators.
Manage accounts, dashboards and coordination permissions.
Prevent duplicates, moderate content and improve community verification.
Maintain security, prevent abuse and improve the service.
Comply with legal obligations when applicable.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis and consent
paragraphs: Browsing the public site does not require registration. When you submit a form, you authorize us to process the data provided for the purposes described.
Creating an account implies express acceptance of this policy and the terms of use.

sections.title: 5. Storage and security
paragraphs: Data is hosted on cloud infrastructure (Supabase/PostgreSQL) with reasonable security measures. No system is 100% infallible; please do not submit unnecessary sensitive information.
We retain data while relevant for the emergency, operation of the service or legal compliance, and may then anonymize or delete it.

sections.title: 6. Cookies and local storage
paragraphs: We use cookies and local storage for user sessions, language/theme preferences and technical features. You can limit cookies in your browser, although some features may stop working correctly.

sections.title: 7. Sharing with third parties
paragraphs: Information you publish in public listings (for example, a help center or citizen report) may be visible to other users.
We do not sell personal data. We may use infrastructure providers (hosting, database, maps) that process data on our behalf under data processing agreements.
We may disclose information if required by law or to protect rights and safety.

sections.title: 8. Your rights
paragraphs: You may request access, correction, updating or deletion of your personal data, or object to certain processing, by contacting StartupVen through startupven.com.
We will respond within a reasonable timeframe. Some data published in an emergency context may require additional verification before being modified or removed.

sections.title: 9. Minors
paragraphs: The platform is not intended for unsupervised minors. If you register information about a minor (for example, in missing person reports), you declare that you have authorization to do so.

sections.title: 10. Changes
paragraphs: We may update this policy. We will publish the current version on this page with the update date. Continued use of the site after changes implies acceptance.

sections.title: 11. Contact
paragraphs: For privacy inquiries, contact StartupVen at startupven.com.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.subtitle: Conditions for using Emergency Center during and after emergencies.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance
paragraphs: By accessing or using Emergency Center you accept these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

sections.title: 2. Nature of the service
paragraphs: Emergency Center is a community information hub. It does not replace official emergency services (171, firefighters, civil protection, hospitals, etc.).
Information can change rapidly during a crisis. Always verify on site and with official sources before acting.

sections.title: 3. Permitted use
paragraphs: You agree to:
list: Use the platform in good faith to coordinate humanitarian aid.
Not publish false, defamatory, fraudulent information or content that puts others at risk.
Not attempt to compromise security, overload the system or extract data in unauthorized bulk.
Respect the privacy and dignity of people, especially in missing persons and damage modules.

sections.title: 4. User content
paragraphs: You are responsible for the accuracy of what you submit. By publishing, you grant us a non-exclusive license to display, store and distribute that content within the service.
We may review, hide or remove content that violates these terms, the law or poses a risk to the community.

sections.title: 5. Community verification
paragraphs: Votes and comments reflect user opinions, not official rulings. Use your judgment and confirm critical data through official channels.

sections.title: 6. Accounts and coordinators
paragraphs: Help center coordinator accounts must only be used to manage real information about the center you represent. You are responsible for keeping your credentials secure.

sections.title: 7. Third-party links and services
paragraphs: The site links to external platforms (VenApp, maps, social networks, etc.). We do not control or guarantee their content or availability.

sections.title: 8. Free and open software
paragraphs: Emergency Center is free and open-source software. The online service is provided «as is», without warranties of continuous availability, absolute accuracy or absence of errors.

sections.title: 9. Limitation of liability
paragraphs: To the extent permitted by law, StartupVen, its collaborators and contributors shall not be liable for direct or indirect damages arising from use or inability to use the platform, or decisions made based on information published here.
In a life-threatening emergency, call official numbers immediately.

sections.title: 10. Modifications
paragraphs: We may modify these terms or site features. The current version will be published on this page.

sections.title: 11. Applicable law
paragraphs: These terms are interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela, without prejudice to mandatory consumer protection rules that may apply.

sections.title: 12. Contact
paragraphs: For legal or service inquiries, contact StartupVen at startupven.com.

footer.openSource: This platform is free and open-source software. You can review it, use it and contribute improvements.
footer.disclaimer: This platform centralizes public information. Always verify with official sources.

hub.title: Collection centers
hub.subtitle: Select a center to manage volunteers and inventory.

registerDialog.title: Register your collection center
registerDialog.subtitle: Complete the form to add another center to your account.
registerDialog.mandatorySubtitle: To use the portal you must register at least one collection center. This step is required.

errors.loadFailed: Could not load data. Check the database connection.
errors.databaseUnavailable: Could not connect to the database. Check DATABASE_URL in .env and that your Supabase project is active.

acceso.title: Portal access
acceso.subtitle: Sign in or create an account to manage your collection center.
acceso.forgotSubtitle: We'll email you a link to reset your password.
acceso.forgotSent: If the email is registered, you'll receive a link shortly.
acceso.registerCenterPrompt: Don't have a center registered yet?

otp.title: Verify your email
otp.subtitle: Enter the code we sent to {email}.
otp.verifiedLogin: Email verified. Sign in to continue.

reset.title: Reset password
reset.subtitle: Choose a new password for your coordinator account.
reset.success: Password updated. You can sign in now.
reset.sessionRequired: The link is invalid or expired. Request a new one.
